eco_baseline_footprint.formula: BIF = (vCPU x 15 EUR) + (Storage GB x 0.08 EUR)
eco_baseline_footprint.method_note: The MCC assumes 4 IBFT validator nodes, normalized as 4 validator-class nodes with 4 vCPUs and 100 GB of persistent storage each. RAM is considered included in compute cost and is not monetized separately in the framework cost model.
eco_baseline_footprint.source: Framework cost model applied to the Quorum Minimum Correctness Configuration (4 IBFT validator nodes)

ps_throughput.estimation_note: Representative benchmark-level estimate for a 4-validator IBFT deployment. Actual performance depends on block size, block period, transaction complexity, gas settings, hardware, and network latency.
ps_throughput.source: GoQuorum IBFT consensus documentation and performance-oriented technical material

eco_variable_transaction_cost.method_note: This metric captures protocol-level per-transaction fees only. In Quorum private networks, gas price can be configured to zero, so no protocol-level monetary fee is required per transaction. Infrastructure and operational costs are captured separately through BIF.

eco_storage_footprint.method_note: Baseline storage footprint for the adopted MCC, assuming full state replication across 4 IBFT validator nodes.
eco_storage_footprint.source: Framework baseline storage assumption for Quorum MCC

parallel_execution.motivation: Quorum does not provide native parallel execution of independent transactions at the protocol level; transactions are executed against a globally shared EVM state.

horizontal_partitioning.motivation: The adopted Quorum baseline does not include native horizontal partitioning or sharding of transaction processing across independent execution domains.

absence_of_global_serialization.motivation: Quorum maintains a single globally ordered blockchain within each network, so transaction ordering is not partitioned across multiple independent ledger domains in the adopted baseline.

scalable_coordination_model.motivation: Compared with proof-of-work blockchains, Quorum's permissioned IBFT-based coordination model reduces coordination overhead by avoiding mining competition and supporting deterministic validator-based consensus.

ps_scalability.source: GoQuorum IBFT architecture and replicated-ledger consensus model

append_only_structural_history.motivation: Quorum maintains blockchain history as an append-only sequence of committed blocks.

cryptographic_linkage_between_records.motivation: Blocks are cryptographically linked to prior blocks and state transitions are recorded on the replicated ledger.

cryptographic_write_authentication.motivation: Transactions are digitally signed in the Ethereum-compatible account model before being accepted by the network.

independent_integrity_verification.motivation: Each validator maintains replicated state and can independently verify ledger integrity under the common protocol rules.

protocol_enforced_irreversible_commit.motivation: Under IBFT, committed blocks are final and are not subject to probabilistic fork-based rollback.

multi_party_confirmation_requirement.motivation: Block commitment requires validator quorum agreement under the IBFT protocol.

absence_of_unilateral_rollback_capabilit.motivation: No single validator or ordinary participant can unilaterally roll back valid committed state.

rci_write_availability_fault_tolerance.configuration_note: This score refers to the native 4-validator IBFT model and does not model additional enterprise HA extensions or geographic redundancy.

absence_of_single_writer_requirement.motivation: Write progress does not depend on a structurally unique primary writer; leadership is protocol-managed within the validator set.

quorum_based_write_continuation.motivation: Write continuation is preserved while the IBFT validator quorum remains available.

automatic_write_continuation_mechanism.motivation: The protocol supports automatic continuation within the fault-tolerance bounds of the validator set, without requiring manual failover.

multi_node_write_survivability.motivation: Write capability survives single-node loss in the replicated 4-validator IBFT configuration.

rci_write_availability_fault_tolerance.source: GoQuorum IBFT quorum-based write continuation model

absence_of_single_entity_unilateral_stat.motivation: No single validator has unilateral authority over valid ledger state; state progression depends on consensus across the validator set.

structural_requirement_for_multi_entity_.motivation: Validation and block commitment are structurally multi-validator under the IBFT protocol.

validator_admission_independence.motivation: Validator admission is permissioned and governed through consortium-level administrative control rather than open protocol-level participation.

absence_of_structurally_fixed_validator_.motivation: Governance participation remains restricted to authorized consortium members rather than being openly distributed across a permissionless participant set.

native_privilege_differentiation_capabil.motivation: Quorum provides native permissioning mechanisms supporting differentiated participation and access control at network and node level.

native_identity_lifecycle_governance_cap.motivation: Permissioned deployments support controlled participant admission, update, and removal procedures.

structural_independence_of_identity_gove.motivation: Identity and access governance remain dependent on consortium authorities and administrative governance structures rather than on a protocol-native distributed identity-governance layer.

ags_collusion_resilience.configuration_note: Specific to the native 4-validator IBFT model adopted in the benchmark.

non_unilateral_compromise_requirement.motivation: A single validator is not sufficient to compromise consensus outcomes or unilaterally alter valid ledger state under the IBFT model.

majority_compromise_required.motivation: Effective compromise requires coordinated control of multiple validators participating in consensus rather than unilateral action by one node.

supermajority_compromise_required.motivation: The adopted 4-validator IBFT model relies on Byzantine fault-tolerance assumptions requiring a supermajority of validators for consensus safety in the sense captured by this framework.

ags_collusion_resilience.source: GoQuorum IBFT fault-tolerance properties operationalized within the framework
